message:
🐛 Fix e2e suite to fall back to in-cluster config on OpenShift runners (#776)

* fix: fall back to in-cluster config when kubeconfig file missing

The e2e test suite hardcodes ~/.kube/config as the default kubeconfig
path, but self-hosted OpenShift runners use in-cluster auth via service
accounts and don't have a kubeconfig file. This causes BeforeSuite to
fail with "stat /home/runner/.kube/config: no such file or directory"
before any tests run.

Check if the kubeconfig file exists first; if not, fall back to
rest.InClusterConfig() which uses the pod's service account token.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: let LLMD_NAMESPACE and MONITORING_NAMESPACE flow through from CI env

The test-e2e-full target was overriding LLMD_NAMESPACE and
MONITORING_NAMESPACE with kind-emulator defaults (llm-d-sim and
workload-variant-autoscaler-monitoring), ignoring the values set by CI
workflows. On OpenShift, this caused BeforeSuite to look for Prometheus
in the wrong namespace and time out.

Remove the overrides from test-e2e-full so CI-set env vars flow through
to go test. For local kind-emulator usage, config.go already defaults
to the correct kind values when these env vars are unset.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Disable scale-to-zero tests on OpenShift

OpenShift's HPAScaleToZero feature gate is not enabled on
pok-prod-sa, so HPA validation rejects minReplicas=0. This
caused 8 test failures: parallel_load, smoke, saturation
(conditional minReplicas=0), and scale_from_zero/scale_to_zero
(hardcoded minReplicas=0).

Set SCALE_TO_ZERO_ENABLED=false in the OCP CI workflow. This
makes the conditional tests use minReplicas=1 and skips the
scale-from-zero/scale-to-zero tests (they already have Skip
guards for !cfg.ScaleToZeroEnabled).

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Auto-disable scale-to-zero on OpenShift in test config

For issue_comment triggers (/ok-to-test), GitHub Actions loads the
workflow YAML from the default branch, not the PR branch. This meant
the SCALE_TO_ZERO_ENABLED env var change in the workflow file was
ignored at runtime.

Move the fix to config.go where it takes effect from PR checkout:
when ENVIRONMENT=openshift, force ScaleToZeroEnabled=false regardless
of the env var, since OpenShift doesn't have HPAScaleToZero enabled.

Also revert the workflow env change since the code handles it.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -269,8 +269,6 @@ test-e2e-full: manifests generate fmt vet ## Run full e2e test suite
 	KUBECONFIG=$(KUBECONFIG) \
 	ENVIRONMENT=$(ENVIRONMENT) \
 	WVA_NAMESPACE=$(CONTROLLER_NAMESPACE) \
-	LLMD_NAMESPACE=$(E2E_EMULATED_LLMD_NAMESPACE) \
-	MONITORING_NAMESPACE=$(E2E_MONITORING_NAMESPACE) \
 	USE_SIMULATOR=$(USE_SIMULATOR) \
 	SCALE_TO_ZERO_ENABLED=$(SCALE_TO_ZERO_ENABLED) \
 	MODEL_ID=$(MODEL_ID) \

@@ -61,8 +61,14 @@ var _ = BeforeSuite(func() {
 
 	By("Initializing Kubernetes client")
 	var err error
-	restConfig, err = clientcmd.BuildConfigFromFlags("", cfg.Kubeconfig)
-	Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "Failed to load kubeconfig")
+	if _, statErr := os.Stat(cfg.Kubeconfig); statErr == nil {
+		restConfig, err = clientcmd.BuildConfigFromFlags("", cfg.Kubeconfig)
+		Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "Failed to load kubeconfig")
+	} else {
+		GinkgoWriter.Printf("Kubeconfig not found at %s, falling back to in-cluster config\n", cfg.Kubeconfig)
+		restConfig, err = rest.InClusterConfig()
+		Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "Failed to load in-cluster config (no kubeconfig file and not running in-cluster)")
+	}
 
 	k8sClient, err = kubernetes.NewForConfig(restConfig)
 	Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred(), "Failed to create Kubernetes clientset")
